skill.name: radiology-image-quiz
skill.description: Generates interactive radiology quizzes with clinical cases across X-ray, CT, MRI, and ultrasound modalities for medical education and board exam preparation.
skill.summary: Create structured radiology quiz questions from clinical case descriptions for medical training and self-assessment.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 49 static analysis findings across 4 files (583 lines). All findings are false positives: 33 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are in markdown documentation code blocks only, not executed commands. 2 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings in scripts/main.py are benign 'argparse' imports. 7 'System reconnaissance' findings are in a legacy audit JSON report (PASS/FAIL results) and documentation about output discipline. 2 'Hardcoded URL' findings are a legitimate GitHub source attribution. The executable scripts/main.py reads JSON case files and generates text-based quizzes with no network access, shell execution, or data exfiltration. The skill is safe for publication.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Commands in Markdown Documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 33 instances of backtick-enclosed commands in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. All instances are inside markdown fenced code blocks (```bash, ```python, ```text) or inline code formatting. They are documentation examples showing CLI usage, audit commands, and Python code snippets. No actual shell execution occurs from these strings.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'argparse' import on line 4 and ArgumentParser constructor on line 38 of scripts/main.py as a 'Weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is a scanner false match - argparse is the standard Python argument parsing library, not a cryptographic function. Additional matches in radiology-image-quiz_audit_result_v2.json:77 and SKILL.md:3,24 are benign text strings in audit data and skill description.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 9 instances as 'System reconnaissance' in radiology-image-quiz_audit_result_v2.json and SKILL.md. The JSON file is a legacy audit result containing PASS/FAIL status values, which are evaluation metadata not reconnaissance. The SKILL.md mentions are documentation about 'output discipline' and 'audit-ready commands' which describe the skill's thoroughness, not actual system probing.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Attribution (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged a URL on SKILL.md line 7 as a 'Hardcoded URL' network indicator. This URL (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills) is the legitimate GitHub source repository link for the skill, used for proper attribution. It is not associated with data exfiltration or suspicious network behavior.

content.user_title: Generate Radiology Image Quizzes for Medical Training
content.value_statement: Medical educators and students need structured quiz content for radiology board exam preparation and clinical skill assessment. This skill generates formatted radiology quizzes with clinical case history, imaging findings, diagnostic options, and explanations across X-ray, CT, MRI, and ultrasound modalities.
actual_capabilities: Generate multiple-choice and open-ended radiology quiz questions from clinical case descriptions
Create quizzes across imaging modalities including X-ray, CT, MRI, and ultrasound
Calibrate question difficulty for medical students, residents, fellows, and attending physicians
Load quiz cases from structured JSON files for reproducible quiz generation
Produce formatted quiz output with case history, findings, answer options, and clinical explanations
Support demo mode for quick testing of quiz format and structure
limitations: Does not display actual medical images - uses text descriptions of imaging findings only
Cannot verify clinical accuracy of user-provided case content - always consult a domain expert
Does not support image upload or DICOM file processing
Output is plain text only, not PDF, HTML, or interactive web formats

use_cases.title: Resident Board Exam Preparation
use_cases.description: A radiology resident preparing for board exams needs structured practice questions. This skill generates formatted quizzes from clinical scenarios to test diagnostic reasoning and pattern recognition.

use_cases.title: Medical Student Radiology Teaching
use_cases.description: A medical educator creates quiz materials for a radiology rotation. The skill produces formatted case-based questions covering common imaging findings across multiple modalities.

use_cases.title: Continuing Medical Education Self-Assessment
use_cases.description: A practicing physician reviews radiology concepts through self-assessment quizzes. The skill generates varied difficulty questions for ongoing professional development.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Demo Quiz
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a demo radiology quiz with 5 questions covering common chest X-ray findings. Include cases about pneumonia, pulmonary edema, pneumothorax, pleural effusion, and atelectasis. Use the demo mode.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to quickly test the skill capabilities with a pre-built demo

prompt_templates.title: Quiz from Specific Clinical Cases
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a radiology quiz from these clinical cases: Case 1: 55-year-old with headaches, CT shows ring-enhancing lesion. Case 2: 70-year-old with abdominal pain, X-ray shows free air under diaphragm. Include multiple choice options and clinical explanations.
prompt_templates.scenario: A medical educator has specific cases and needs a formatted quiz

prompt_templates.title: Custom Difficulty Board Exam Quiz
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 10 multiple choice questions at resident difficulty level about emergency radiology. Cover CT findings for aortic dissection, acute stroke, and spinal cord compression. Include differential diagnosis options.
prompt_templates.scenario: A resident needs advanced board exam practice questions

prompt_templates.title: Bulk Quiz from JSON Case File
prompt_templates.prompt: Load cases from a JSON file and generate a comprehensive quiz. Each case entry has history, findings, 4 answer options, correct answer, and explanation. Format all cases with numbering and separated sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: An educator has a pre-made case database in JSON format for batch quiz generation

output_examples.input: Generate a quiz for a 65-year-old male with acute chest pain, CT shows peripheral wedge-shaped opacity in the right lower lobe.
output_examples.output: The skill generates a formatted case with history summary, imaging findings description, 4 multiple choice answer options (Pulmonary embolism, Pneumonia, Lung cancer, Atelectasis), the correct answer with rationale, and a clinical explanation referencing the classic imaging pattern.

output_examples.input: Create a beginner-level radiology quiz for medical students covering 3 basic chest X-ray cases.
output_examples.output: The skill produces 3 formatted quiz cases with simplified language, basic clinical histories, clear findings descriptions, single-best-answer multiple choice questions, and educational explanations appropriate for early medical training.

output_examples.input: Generate an advanced trauma radiology quiz for emergency medicine fellows.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a multi-case quiz with complex clinical presentations, detailed CT findings, multiple differential diagnosis options per case, and management-oriented explanations suitable for advanced clinical training.

best_practices: Prepare complete case content with clear history, specific imaging findings, and the correct diagnosis before generating quizzes to ensure meaningful educational value.
Choose the difficulty level that matches the target audience: medical student for basic patterns, resident for intermediate cases, and fellow or attending for rare or complex findings.
Use the JSON file input method for batch quiz generation when you have a pre-existing case database to ensure reproducible and consistent output across study sessions.
anti_patterns: Generating quizzes about imaging modalities not supported by the skill (only X-ray, CT, MRI, and ultrasound are covered). Other modalities may produce inaccurate educational content.
Using this skill to provide clinical diagnostic recommendations. The tool is designed for educational quiz creation only, not for clinical decision support or patient care.
Providing incomplete case data without history, findings, or answer explanations. Missing fields reduce the educational value and may produce confusing quiz output.

faq.question: What imaging modalities does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports X-ray, CT, MRI, and ultrasound modalities. Other modalities such as nuclear medicine or fluoroscopy are not covered.

faq.question: Can I use my own radiology images with this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses text descriptions of imaging findings only. It does not process actual image files, DICOM data, or image uploads.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for board exam preparation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill creates structured quizzes with case history, imaging findings, answer options, and clinical explanations suitable for board exam practice.

faq.question: How do I generate a quiz from my own data?
faq.answer: Prepare a JSON file where each case entry includes history, findings, options, answer, and explanation fields. Use the --cases flag to load the file.

faq.question: Can this skill verify the medical accuracy of my quiz content?
faq.answer: No. Clinical accuracy depends entirely on the user-provided content. Always verify quiz content with a qualified domain expert before use.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Output is plain text formatted with case numbering and section separators. The skill does not generate PDF, HTML, or interactive web formats.
